questions.question: Differentiate between informed and uninformed search strategies with examples.
keyPoints: Uninformed/Blind search uses no domain knowledge (BFS, DFS, UCS)
Informed/Heuristic search uses problem-specific knowledge (A*, Greedy)
Informed search is generally more efficient

questions.question: Explain the properties of A* search algorithm and conditions for its optimality.
keyPoints: Optimal if heuristic is admissible (never overestimates)

questions.question: What is the minimax algorithm? Explain with a proper example.
keyPoints: MAX player maximizes score, MIN minimizes
Terminal positions evaluated by utility function

questions.question: Discuss the advantages and disadvantages of Hill Climbing search.
keyPoints: Disadvantages: Local maxima problem, ridge/plateau issues, not complete or optimal

questions.question: Explain Alpha-Beta pruning with an example tree showing which nodes get pruned.
Compare BFS and DFS search algorithms in terms of completeness, optimality, time and space complexity.
keyPoints: BFS: Complete, Optimal for unweighted, O(b^d) time, O(b^d) space
DFS: Not complete, Not optimal, O(b^m) time, O(bm) space
BFS uses queue, DFS uses stack
BFS good for shortest path, DFS for solution depth

questions.question: What are admissible heuristics? Give examples for the 8-puzzle problem.
keyPoints: Admissible: Never overestimates true cost (h(n) <= h*(n))
- Number of tiles out of row/column (not admissible)

questions.question: Explain the A* search algorithm in detail. Show how it combines the advantages of UCS and Greedy Best-First search. Also prove that A* is optimal when the heuristic is admissible.
keyPoints: g(n): actual cost from start to n
h(n): estimated cost from n to goal
Optimality proof: No other optimal path can have lower f-cost
If h is admissible, A* never misses optimal solution
Consistency ensures f-cost never decreases along path

questions.question: Design and implement a solution for the 8-puzzle problem using A* search. Explain the heuristic functions and compare performance using different heuristics.
keyPoints: Goal state: tiles 1-8 in order with blank at end
Performance comparison: dominated vs dominant heuristics

questions.question: Explain the Minimax algorithm for game playing. Discuss how Alpha-Beta pruning improves the efficiency of Minimax with suitable examples.
keyPoints: Terminal states evaluated by utility function
Alpha: MAX's best value on path to root
Beta: MIN's best value on path to root
Pruning condition: if alpha >= beta, prune
Worst case O(b^d), best case O(b^(d/2))

questions.question: Compare and contrast various uninformed search strategies: BFS, DFS, Uniform Cost Search, Depth-Limited Search, and Iterative Deepening. Include analysis of time/space complexity, completeness, and optimality.
keyPoints: DFS: Incomplete for infinite, Optimal (if shallowest), O(bm) space
UCS: Complete, Optimal (weighted), O(b^d) time, O(b^d) space
DLS: Complete (if L>=d*), Not optimal if L<d*, O(b^L) space
IDDFS: Complete, Optimal (if L=d*), Combines BFS+DFS advantages
